svg.text: TIBETAN
HISTORY & THE HIMALAYAN WORLD
TIBETAN EMPIRE
~618-842 CE: Tibetan Empire; one of Asia's great powers
Songtsen Gampo: unified Tibet, created script, married Nepali princess!
Bhrikuti: Nepali princess brought Buddhism to Tibet from Nepal
763 CE: Tibetan armies briefly occupy Chang'an (Chinese capital)
842 CE: Imperial collapse; fragmentation into petty kingdoms
BUDDHISM AND TIBETAN CULTURE
7th c.: Buddhism enters Tibet from India and Nepal
Padmasambhava: brought Vajrayana Buddhism; Tibetan Buddhist canon
~6M speakers; Buddhist scripture preserves vast Indian knowledge
Kangyur + Tengyur: 100+ volumes of translated Buddhist texts
NEPALI HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
NEPAL AS HIMALAYAN CROSSROADS
Kathmandu Valley: ancient trade route between India and Tibet
Licchavi dynasty (400-750 CE): Nepal as cultural bridge state
Bhrikuti: princess who took Buddhism north to Tibet (~630 CE)
Newari: indigenous language of Kathmandu valley; different from Nepali
GORKHA KINGDOM & PRITHVI NARAYAN SHAH
1768: Prithvi Narayan Shah unifies Nepal; Nepali becomes national language
Nepali (Gorkhali): spread by Gurkha soldiers across Himalayan region
Gurkha regiments: served British Empire; took Nepali language across Asia
NEPALI TODAY
~17M speakers; official language of Nepal
Also spoken in Darjeeling, Sikkim, Bhutan, and by diaspora
Scheduled language of India (Sikkim); recognized in Bhutan
HIMALAYAN BOND: TIBET & NEPAL
Mount Everest / Sagarmatha / Chomolungma: named in both languages
Nepal: buffer between India and Tibet; always both Buddhist and Hindu
Lhasa-Kathmandu trade route: centuries of wool, salt, grain exchange
Tibetan refugees: 20,000+ in Nepal since 1959 (Dalai Lama exile)
Two mountain peoples: shaped by altitude, Buddhism, trade
PLATE 56d ALT · TIBETAN / NEPALI · HISTORY
